chunks.text: Department Policy on Academic Misconduct - EECS at Berkeley Department Policy on Academic Misconduct Copying all or part of another person’s work or using reference materials not specifically allowed are forms of academic misconduct that will not be tolerated at UC Berkeley and in EECS. If we find that you were involved in an incident of academic misconduct, your instructor will notify you. Below you will find the departmental policy on academic dishonesty. For more information, refer to the Center for Student Conduct Academic Integrity web page. The instructor may take actions including: Requiring repetition of the course work Assigning an F grade or a “zero” grade to the subject work, and, for serious offenses (such as academic misconduct on an exam), assigning an F grade to the course. The recommended action for academic misconduct on examinations, term papers or projects is assigning an F grade to the course. The instructor should inform the Center for Student Conduct of the incident using a Faculty Disposition Form . The student should be made aware of the right of the student to appeal to the Department Grievance Committee the actions taken by the instructor or to have the matter resolved by the Center for Student Conduct . The instructor must retain copies of any written evidence or observation notes. The Center for Student Conduct offers the student both the opportunity to resolve the incident informally or by a formal hearing process in determining whether there has been a violation of the Code

Center for Student Conduct . The instructor must retain copies of any written evidence or observation notes. The Center for Student Conduct offers the student both the opportunity to resolve the incident informally or by a formal hearing process in determining whether there has been a violation of the Code of Student Conduct and any sanctions that may follow. The Department will recommend that students involved in a second incident of academic misconduct be dismissed from the University.
